purpose: Define the storage, review, governance, and migration gates required before Agent Lab memory, artifacts, audit logs, gates, source candidates, and source claims can move from prototype-local persistence to enterprise storage.

requirements.label: Browser-local session ledger
acceptanceCriteria: Agent Lab can merge turns into a browser-local ledger.
Reloaded sessions preserve memory, artifacts, audit, confirmation gates, and reviews.
requirements.nextAction: Keep browser-local storage as a convenience cache, not the enterprise source of truth.
guardrails: Browser-local storage is not enterprise persistence.

requirements.label: Local JSON session store
acceptanceCriteria: Turns with a sessionId persist to local JSON.
Session ledger API can read and merge persisted ledgers.
blockers: Local JSON is ignored prototype storage and is not enterprise database persistence.
requirements.nextAction: Use local JSON for prototype continuity while enterprise persistence requirements are defined.
guardrails: Do not treat local JSON records as enterprise-approved records.

requirements.label: Reviewed actions workflow
acceptanceCriteria: Memory can be accepted, edited, or rejected.
Artifacts can be approved for prototype use without enabling export.
Blocked capability gates cannot be approved.
requirements.nextAction: Preserve review history when moving to enterprise storage.
guardrails: Human review actions do not bypass disabled capability gates.

requirements.label: Accepted memory context injection
acceptanceCriteria: Only accepted active-brand memory loads into future turns.
Suggested, rejected, and blocked memory remains excluded.
requirements.nextAction: Keep accepted memory scoped by brand and review status.
guardrails: Suggested memory must never auto-promote into working context.

requirements.label: Source candidates stay non-canonical
acceptanceCriteria: Reviewed-local source packets are visible as candidates.
Extracted source claims are visible as review records.
Canonical writes and runtime auto-consumption remain disabled.
requirements.nextAction: Define canonical promotion workflow before any source candidate becomes runtime evidence.
guardrails: Reviewed-local source candidates and claims are not packet facts.

requirements.label: Enterprise database schema
acceptanceCriteria: Enterprise tables or document stores exist for all runtime record types.
Schema preserves relationships between turns, evidence, gates, reviews, artifacts, and source candidates.
blockers: No enterprise database provider or schema is connected for session memory, artifacts, audit, gates, reviews, source candidates, or source claims.
requirements.nextAction: Design the enterprise persistence schema after pilot governance confirms required record types and retention rules.
guardrails: Do not migrate prototype records into enterprise truth without schema and owner approval.

requirements.label: Reviewer identity and access control
acceptanceCriteria: Reviewer identity is tied to enterprise auth.
Brand/category access rules are enforced.
Review actions record accountable user and role.
blockers: Current prototype records reviewer as human_review without enterprise identity or role-based access control.
requirements.nextAction: Connect review workflows to enterprise identity before treating reviews as official approvals.
guardrails: Prototype review identity is not sufficient for enterprise approvals.

requirements.label: Retention and privacy policy
acceptanceCriteria: Retention duration is approved.
Deletion and export rules are defined.
Voice transcripts and pasted source text have approved handling.
blockers: Retention, deletion, and transcript/source-text handling are not yet approved.
requirements.nextAction: Define retention and privacy rules before enterprise persistence or continuous voice transcript storage.
guardrails: Do not store voice transcripts or pasted source text in enterprise systems without approved retention rules.

requirements.label: Backup, recovery, and migration
acceptanceCriteria: Enterprise storage has backup and recovery behavior.
Prototype-local records have an approved migration or discard policy.
No unsupported local records are silently promoted.
blockers: No enterprise backup/recovery or local-record migration policy exists yet.
requirements.nextAction: Write the operations runbook before migrating local JSON records.
guardrails: Local prototype records require review before migration or deletion.

requirements.label: Canonical source promotion governance
acceptanceCriteria: Source-owner approvals define which records can become canonical.
Runtime auto-consumption rules are approved.
Promotion actions are reversible and audited.
blockers: Source data writes and source claim promotion capabilities remain disabled.
requirements.nextAction: Define source-owner promotion workflow before enabling canonical writes or runtime auto-consumption.
guardrails: Review gates are review state only until canonical promotion governance is approved.
Prototype persistence can support demos and review workflows, but it is not enterprise storage.
Accepted memory, reviewed artifacts, source candidates, and extracted claims must remain auditable, reversible, and scoped.
Canonical writes, runtime auto-consumption, and enterprise promotion remain disabled until governance approves them.
caveats: This registry defines readiness for persistence promotion; it does not create enterprise storage.
Local JSON records under .runtime are ignored prototype state and should not be treated as official source truth.
